description: One strand and the whole growth can both be 'whila', the fine hair extending from human or animal skin. 'koma whila' specifies head hair, and the registered compound 'nolika whila' (animal hair) is fur when that distinction matters.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for unvoiced 'wh', then move forward and unround into stressed high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and open into central unrounded 'a'.
usage_notes: Context supplies the difference between one hair and hair as a mass. A preceding body-part noun identifies where it grows.

semantic_domains.physical: fine strands growing from human or animal skin
